data.title: Ticket system: closed story accepts new children and closes with open children
data.description:
## Problem

Story 2932-51a6 (dso:ui-designer agent) is closed, but has 5 open child tasks:
- ab85-baf6: Write failing tests for dso:ui-designer agent contract
- faa0-cb2c: Create ui-designer return payload contract document
- f02d-e3dc: Create dso:ui-designer agent definition
- 08a3-cae0: Add design_creation routing entry and update tests
- 79b0-ce0b: Fix pre-existing EXPECTED_CATEGORIES drift

All 5 have child-of links to 2932-51a6 via the dependency system.

## Expected Behavior

Two guards should exist:
1. **Close guard**: Transitioning a story to closed should be blocked when open child tasks exist (same pattern as epic closure blocking on open stories).
2. **Add-child guard**: Adding a child-of link to an already-closed parent should be rejected (or at minimum warn), since the child will never be executed as part of that story's sprint batch.

## Actual Behavior

Neither guard exists. The ticket CLI allows both operations silently, resulting in orphaned open tasks under a closed parent that will never be picked up by sprint or debug-everything.

## Scope

Investigate whether the ticket-transition and ticket-link scripts enforce parent-child lifecycle constraints. The epic closure guard (blocked while children are open) suggests the pattern exists at the epic level but was never implemented for stories.
